mechanics.name: Prepared
mechanics.summary: A creature-front DFC that lets you cast a free copy of its back-face spell while prepared.

mechanics.name: Repartee
mechanics.summary: Triggers when you cast an instant/sorcery targeting a creature — the Silverquill counters engine.

mechanics.name: Infusion
mechanics.summary: Bonus when you gained life this turn — the Witherbloom lifegain payoff.

mechanics.name: Opus
mechanics.summary: Rewards casting instants/sorceries, with a bonus if five or more mana was spent.

mechanics.name: Increment
mechanics.summary: Put a +1/+1 counter whenever a spell's mana spent exceeds this creature's power or toughness.

mechanics.name: Flashback
mechanics.summary: Cast from the graveyard, then exile — feeds Lorehold's graveyard-leaves payoffs.

archetypes.name: Silverquill drain
archetypes.playstyle: Cheap creature-targeting spells double as Repartee fuel while drain bodies close.

archetypes.name: Lorehold graveyard
archetypes.playstyle: Fill the graveyard, then make cards leave it to fire the payoffs while Spirits go wide.
signposts: Kirol, History Buff // Pack a Punch

archetypes.name: Prismari spells
archetypes.playstyle: Chain instants and sorceries with ritual mana to pay the Opus five-mana kickers.

archetypes.name: Quandrix counters
archetypes.playstyle: Ramp so every spell overspends its cost, snowballing Increment and Fractal counters.

archetypes.name: Witherbloom lifegain
archetypes.playstyle: Gain life every turn with Pest tokens and ETB drains to switch on the Infusion payoffs.

combos.note: A cheap creature-targeting spell triggers Repartee counters
Exiling a card from your graveyard for mana fires graveyard-leaves payoffs
Gaining life makes the Infusion trample/indestructible live
Ritual mana pushes the Opus five-mana kicker
Ramp makes each spell overspend and grow Increment
Attacking Pests gain life that becomes counters
